title: Creativity Furnace
description: Lightweight utility modpack for Creative+ genre multiplayer servers.
body:
# What is C+? **C+** (stands for Creative+) is a *nieche* servers genre, which (in most cases) gives you the opportunity to create your own minigames with something kinda like code environment. Take a look at the most popular ones — you might be familiar with some of them:
| Logo | Name | Version | Language | Core | | :--------: | :-----------:| :-----: | :---------: | :------: | | DiamondFire | 1.21.1 | English | Proprietary
([Github](https://github.com/MCDiamondFire)) | | | JustMC | 1.21.1 | Russian | Proprietary
([Gitlab](https://gitlab.com/justmc/justmc/)) | | | Eagle's C+ | 1.21.4 | Russian | Open-source
([Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/plugin/opencreative)) | Well, as you can see, there aren't many of them...
Actually there's like 10 of them, but most of are made specifically to create money, so they're not listed.
Note: This modpack is not affiliated with or sponsored by any of them. # Why 1.21.4? Well, there is a reason. 1. Some essential mods *(about this later)* are only present before 1.21.4 *(or 1.21.4 exactly)*, which is unfortunate, but, well, what could we do here? 2. 1.21.4 is stable and new enough version to play on. # Mods There won't be listed very well-known mods (e.g. [Sodium](https://modrinth.com/mod/sodium)) won't be listed here — come on, everyone knows them... So, here's less known ones, but they're essential for your... eh... *game dev..?* I don't even know what to call it.

Lets begin with something you're going to do everytime creating a new minigame: designing.
This might be text, items (for GUIs) etc. Following mods were added for this reason scpecifically:
| Name | Description | Link | | :------------: | :------------------------------------------------: | :--------: | | NBT Editor | Can edit item NBT data with user-friendly interface | [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/nbt-editor) | | FZMM | Can create gradient text, edit heads etc. | [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/fzmm) | | Symbol Chat | Adds special button in chat with various symbols;
Also can change your text to small one. | [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/symbol-chat) |

Next up is other utility mods. They might be used in some cases, but much less often than desing ones:

| Name | Description | Link | | :------------: | :-----------------------------------------------------: | :---------: | | Jade | Shows pop-up in top screen with block\entity data;
Configured to be less obstructuive. | [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/jade) | | clientcommands | Complex mod with a lot of features..
About it in particular - later. | [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/client-commands) | Litematica | Can create hologram with build, and can store building in file. | [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/litematica) | WorldEdit | In case you need to build something, it will be easier to do so
in singleplayer with WE rather than in multiplayer. | [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/plugin/worldedit)

Finally, some ambience mods to enchance game experience:

| Name | Description | Link | | :------------------: | :------------------------------------------------------: | :----------: | | Cherised Worlds | With it, you can pin singleplayer worlds and servers. | [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/cherished-worlds) | | Continuity | Makes glass and other block continuable. | [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/continuity) | | Shulker Box Tooltip | Shows shulker containment in GUI. | [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/shulkerboxtooltip) | | Visuality | Adds more particles to some blocks and fluids. | [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/visuality) | | Tiny item animations | Makes moving items in GUIs more immersive | [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/tiny-item-animations) | Wakes | Enchanses water behaviour a little bit | [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/wakes) |
Well, surely that's not all mods. Download the modpack by itself to check. # Other things to mention This modpack includes some mod configurations designed to make certain mods easier to use and less intrusive.
Oh, I forgot to tell about **clientcommands**: Since I didn't have enough room to explain it in the markdown table... here are some plain text why should you use it:
1. Some servers limit `/give` command, so you can give yourself an item using this command:
`/cgive []`; 2. You can enable fullbright with this command: `/cgamma 15`; 3. Final reason: It also has a built-in Minesweeper game.

Unsure what should I also say here.. Oh, texture packs! They do exist. Even though they're not necessary, they can enhance the gameplay experience a bit more.

# End Thanks for reading this massive markdown file — it was a struggle to write with my *perfect* English skills.
